case_title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS.CO.

docket_entries.description: BALANCE OF FEE PAID FOR TRANSACTION R0910203F009 BY DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS.CO.
SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AS TO DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS.CO. JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
PAYER NOTIFIED OF PARTIAL PAYMENT OF FEE
OPPOSITION TO MTN BY REAL PARY IN INTEREST TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS.CO. HEARING SET FOR MAR-08-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS.CO. $150,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $370.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $150,370.00, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS. CO. MOTION FOR ORDER TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED. THE COURT RULES THERE WAS NOT AN EXTENSION; THERE WAS A TOLLING. THIRTY NINE DAYS ARE STILL REMAINING, TOLLING DATE COMMENCES ON THE DATE THAT THE COURT'S ORDER REGARDING TODAY'S RULING IS ENTERED. SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS VACATED AS PREMATURE. COUNSEL IS ADVISED TO ADD THE MATTER TO THE COURT'S CRIMINAL CALENDAR RE: BOND ISSUES AND ANY OTHER ISSUES DEALING THE CRIMINAL DOCKET. DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER AND HAVE IT APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
SUPPLEMENTAL DOCUMENTS RE: MOTION TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, VACATE FORFEITURE AND EXONERATE BOND OR FOR ALTERNATIVE RELIEF
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAR-08-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INS. CO. Motion for Order to vacate summary judgment. Appearances: Leonor Noguez-Ward, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3854 appearing for Plaintiff; Marc D. Chasman, Esq. Attorney at Law, (818) 788-2245 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Hearing held and argued. The Court rules there was not an extension; there was a tolling. Thirty nine days are still remaining, tolling date commences on the date that the Court's order regarding today's ruling is entered. Summary Judgment is vacated as premature. Counsel is advised to add the matter to the Court's criminal calendar re: Bond issues and any other issues dealing the criminal docket. Defense counsel will prepare Order and have it approved as to form. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
